summary: Every one of the four winner cells in each of C championship rows can alter a title count, and K reported champions must be emitted; normalization, indexed player lookup, and aggregation match this linear bound.

optimality.argument: An exact result must inspect all four winner cells of every championship row because changing any one can change two player counts, which is Omega(C) work for a fixed four tournaments. It must also emit K champion rows. UNION ALL creates exactly 4C winner rows, primary-key player lookups and hash aggregation take constant expected work per normalized row, and emitting the K groups gives O(C + K) total logical time.

replacement_checks.evidence: Every championship winner cell can independently change the required count, and the query must return every one of the K distinct winning players.
Four fixed UNION ALL branches produce 4C rows; each receives one primary-key player lookup and one constant expected-time group update before K rows are emitted.

Authored fixtures cover all four tournament columns, clean sweeps, distinct winners, tied totals, repeated winners across years, noncontiguous identifiers, unsorted players, and exclusion of players with no titles.
